codeReview.summary: AgentPool implementation correctly provides register/reserve/release/available_count operations. Thread-safety is achieved via Arc<RwLock<AgentPool>> wrapper in Orchestrator. MAX_CONCURRENT_AGENTS=6 constant is defined and used by ExecutionController.

issues.description: AgentPool struct itself is not internally thread-safe (uses bare HashMap with &mut self methods). However, this is acceptable since usage is through Arc<RwLock<AgentPool>> in Orchestrator (lib.rs:323). The RwLock provides the necessary synchronization.

MAX_CONCURRENT_AGENTS=6 is defined as a constant but there is no test verifying enforcement of this limit. The value is used by ExecutionController to set max_concurrent field, but AgentPool itself doesn't enforce any capacity limit during registration.

AgentPool tests (test_agent_pool_registration, test_agent_pool_reserve, test_agent_pool_release) verify basic functionality but do not test concurrent access patterns or MAX_CONCURRENT_AGENTS enforcement.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AgentPool is designed to be used behind a RwLock wrapper (Arc<RwLock<AgentPool>>) rather than being internally thread-safe. This pattern is documented in lib.rs but not explicitly in the AgentPool code itself. Workers using AgentPool should access it through the Orchestrator's agent_pool field, not create standalone AgentPool instances.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: lib.rs:323 'agent_pool: Arc<RwLock<AgentPool>>' and usage in execution.rs shows Arc<RwLock<AgentPool>> pattern, but agents.rs AgentPool impl has no internal synchronization

sharedStateObservations.observation: MAX_CONCURRENT_AGENTS constant is defined in lib.rs but the architecture.md in .factory/library doesn't document this constant or the ExecutionController's role in enforcing agent concurrency limits.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: MAX_CONCURRENT_AGENTS defined at lib.rs:55 but .factory/library/architecture.md doesn't mention agent concurrency limits

summary: AgentPool implementation is correct and passes review. The four required operations (register/reserve/release/available_count) are properly implemented. Thread-safety is achieved via RwLock wrapper pattern. MAX_CONCURRENT_AGENTS=6 is defined and used. No blocking issues found - only minor non-blocking observations about test coverage for concurrency limits.
